name: Ananya Deshmukh
tagline: Has used the same face wash since college and will not switch without a very good reason

demographics.occupation: Senior Architect at a design studio in Bandra
demographics.family_status: Married, no kids yet, lives with husband

aspiration_drivers: aesthetic consistency in everything she uses
brands that feel like part of her identity
quality that does not surprise — in a bad way
trust_signals: brands she has personally used for years
recommendations from close friends with similar taste
consistent packaging and messaging over time
rejection_triggers: new brands with no track record
aggressive discount positioning that feels cheap
brands that change their formulation or packaging frequently
emotional_hooks: 'this has never let me down' narrative

backstory: Ananya grew up watching her mother use the same Lakme products for decades. She absorbed the lesson that a brand that works for you is a relationship, not a transaction. At her design studio she applies the same philosophy — she uses the same pen brand, the same notebook, the same coffee. She is not closed to new things, but the new thing has to prove itself against something she already trusts. She has tried maybe two new skincare brands in the last five years.
current_life_context: Her go-to face serum was recently reformulated and she is mildly annoyed. She might — just might — be open to trying something new, but it has to feel trustworthy, premium, and not pushy.
inner_monologue_style: Quietly evaluative. Does not overthink but has a strong gut sense. Thinks: 'I have not heard of this brand, so why would I switch?' or 'This looks clean but is it going to be around in two years or is this another startup that will disappear?'
